feed.title: [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets

entry.title: [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets
entry.summary:
The proposed BIP (Bitcoin Improvement Proposal) introduces a hybrid key derivation scheme that combines the security of SPHINCS+ with the widely used secp256k1 to create quantum-resistant Bitcoin wallets. The title of the proposal is "Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Bitcoin Wallets." This method involves using the NIST-standardized SLH-DSA, known as SPHINCS+, in conjunction with the 3’s Company HE-SD algorithm, which utilizes SPX-QEC for the cleanup process. The primary aim is to derive standard secp256k1 private keys and Bitcoin addresses, including both legacy formats and BIP-341 Taproot addresses.
The beauty of this approach lies in its simplicity and feasibility on low-end hardware such as Raspberry Pi, making it accessible for immediate use without requiring any consensus changes within the Bitcoin network. It ensures compatibility with current Bitcoin software like Electrum, bitaddress.org, and Bitcoin Core. Furthermore, the entire process can be conducted offline, promoting enhanced security by reducing exposure to potential online threats.
For those interested in further details or implementation guidance, resources and code are readily available. The reference implementation along with the full source code can be accessed at [GitHub](https://github.com/DigiMancer3D/bip-xxx-sphincs-hybrid), while the draft text of the BIP is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/DigiMancer3D/bip-xxx-sphincs-hybrid/blob/main/bip-xxx.mediawiki). This initiative not only serves as an innovative solution for quantum-hardened cold storage and paper wallets but also acts as a practical transitional measure while the broader Bitcoin community gradually moves towards a post-quantum consensus.
